seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Garden Island and Gascoyne Junction. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Garden Island vs Gascoyne Junction: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Garden Island and Gascoyne Junction.
heading: Garden Island vs Gascoyne Junction — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Garden Island, Western Australia and Gascoyne Junction, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gascoyne Junction has a lower overall climate risk score (26/100) compared to Garden Island (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Garden Island or Gascoyne Junction safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Garden Island has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Gascoyne Junction scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Garden Island scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Gascoyne Junction scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Garden Island or Gascoyne Junction?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gascoyne Junction has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
